repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

body:
Problem:
`:connect` required an address argument; there was no built-in way to choose from discovered peer servers interactively.

Solution:
`:connect` without an address now presents a `vim.ui.select()` picker of peer servers from `serverlist({ peer = true })`, excluding the current instance's own addresses. `E5768` is reported if no peers are found. `E5769` is reported if no UI is attached. `!` and all command modifiers are forwarded to the follow-up `:connect {address}` call.

To support this, extract `cmdmods_dict()` into `ex_docmd.c` so `nvim_parse_cmd()` and `ex_connect()` share the same command-modifier serialization logic.

Peer discovery only covers `serverlist()` addresses; TCP listeners are not discovered, and Windows cross-process peer discovery is not yet supported.

Refs #5035
AI-assisted: Codex, Claude Code

comment_data.body: could live in `src/nvim/ex_docmd.c` . not `inline` in a header.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -0,0 +1,51 @@
+#pragma once
+
+#include "nvim/api/private/helpers.h"
+#include "nvim/ex_cmds_defs.h"
+#include "nvim/window.h"
+
+static inline Dict cmdmods_dict(const cmdmod_T *cmod, Arena *arena)

comment_data.body: Now that we have `nlua_call_excmd` (see https://github.com/neovim/neovim/pull/39194/ for examples), this code should be much simpler, and it's no longer necessary to move `cmdmods_dict`

Thanks, that makes sense. I’ll rework this now that #39194 has landed: use `nlua_call_excmd()` for the no-arg path and drop the `cmdmods_dict()` changes.

I also saw your comment in #39378 about adding `serverlist({ info = true })` first. I’ll split that into a small prerequisite PR, then rebase this on top so the picker can just use the structured server info.

> Thanks, that makes sense. I’ll rework this now that #39194 has landed: use `nlua_call_excmd()` for the no-arg path and drop the `cmdmods_dict()` changes.
> 
> I also saw your comment in #39378 about adding `serverlist({ info = true })` first. I’ll split that into a small prerequisite PR, then rebase this on top so the picker can just use the structured server info.

Hey, I actually just finished implementing that exact `serverlist({info=v:true})`. I've already opened the PR for it here: #39392.

Hope this saves you some time!

Nice, thanks! That does save me time.

I’ll use #39392 as the prerequisite and rework #38454 on top of it, keeping this PR focused on the no-arg `:connect` picker.

comment_regular_data.body: looks roughly like the right idea but needs cleanup.

Addressed feedback: moved `cmdmods_dict()` into `ex_docmd.c` and removed the separate inline header. Also moved the no-UI check from Lua into C via `ui_active()`, changed the Lua helper to return integer status codes, and fixed the related test failures.

still needs more cleanup. i have my own AI, i don't need yours. i need you to guide your AI to do high-quality work. 

for example, the test code appears to be rather verbose.

review https://github.com/neovim/neovim/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#ai-assisted-work
Tightened the patch and cleaned up the tests.
what's an example of how the connect menu looks?
Here’s how it looks in the current version of this PR:

ok. it's good to do things incrementally, but just for reference the end goal is for the serverlist to be useful/intuitive so that users can choose their intended target without having to remember obscure addresses.

I wrote more details here:

- https://github.com/neovim/neovim/issues/39420
This has been closed since a request for information has not been answered for 30 days. It can be reopened when the requested information is provided.
